data_gaps: red-zone target/carry share and designed-run vs scramble split ARE now retrievable (D-017) and used above -- this is the v1.1 data delta vs v1.0
injury status unavailable in frozen world
Justin Fields absent from NYJ depth chart (captured 2025-02-20, pre-free-agency) -- 2025 starter status unverifiable from tools
J.J. McCarthy: no NFL stats (2024 season missed) -- rookie-equivalent blind spot, confidence capped

rankings.rationale: The pool's best dual-volume profile: 474 attempts PLUS 139 carries for 915 rushing yards, sustained late (55 carries, 410 yards over the last 8 at 25.6 PPG). Rushing volume is the most valuable per-play opportunity a QB can have, and his is elite and stable.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Carries per game dropping below 6 across Weeks 1-3.

rankings.rationale: 483 attempts plus 102 carries with 12 rushing TDs -- goal-line rush usage no other passer matches -- and a last-8 surge to 27.5 PPG on rising attempt volume. I regress the TD rate but the dual volume floor is the league's safest.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Goal-line rushes going to a RB in Weeks 1-3, breaking the 12-TD pattern's opportunity basis.

rankings.rationale: UP from v1.0 #4: designed_run_rate 0.70 and a pool-leading 0.386 red-zone carry share with 14 rushing TDs -- his rushing is scheme-designed AND goal-line-concentrated, the single most secure and repeatable QB rushing profile in the pool, not scramble variance. The 41.9%-pass offense still caps the passing side, but the rushing floor now clearly outranks Daniels'.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: The tush-push/goal-line role producing under 8 rush TDs pace, or attempts falling below 20/game.

rankings.rationale: DOWN from v1.0 #3: 148 carries and 891 rushing yards, but only 6 rushing TDs and a designed_run_rate of 0.48 -- his rushing is yardage-and-scramble driven, valuable but less TD-secure than Hurts' designed goal-line role. Elite dual volume still, just correctly ordered behind Hurts on the new red-zone/rush-type evidence.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Designed rush volume below 5 carries/game early, signaling a protect-the-QB shift.

rankings.rationale: 652 attempts -- the pool's highest passing volume by 70+ -- in the pool's most pass-leaning offense (63.2%), at 24.4 PPG over the last 8. Near-zero rush value means his floor depends entirely on that attempt volume persisting, which the offense's structure supports.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Team pass rate dropping below 58% under a healthier run game.

rankings.rationale: 567 attempts plus 92 carries as a rookie with a rising late window (20.8 PPG) -- second-year volume with the same play-caller is the stable base case. The dual usage separates him from the pure pocket tier below.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Rush attempts below 4/game early, or a run-lean shift under 52% pass.

rankings.rationale: 570 attempts in a 62-plays/game offense is a top-tier volume base; the 41 passing TDs are efficiency my rules regress hard, which is exactly the gap between his 21.5 PPG and this projection. Volume itself returns intact.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Attempt volume below 32/game while the TD rate also reverts.

rankings.rationale: 541 attempts plus 78 carries for 572 yards -- true dual volume across a full 17 games, with a modestly rising late window (18.3 PPG). The offense's 59.2 plays/game is the cap; the rush floor is the reason he clears the pocket passers behind him.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Rush volume below 4 carries/game across the first month.

rankings.rationale: 539 attempts at 23.2 PPG over the last 8 in the pool's highest-volume offense (63.8 plays/game) -- but literally zero rush value (56 yards, 0 TDs), so the entire projection rides on pass volume and efficiency I partially regress. A new coordinator is the stated play-volume unknown.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Detroit's play volume or pass rate dropping materially under the new coordinator.

rankings.rationale: 581 attempts at 99.3% snap share -- volume as secure as anyone's -- with modest rush usage and a 17.7 PPG output that already reflects role-average efficiency. KC's 163 vacated targets imply receiver churn, not QB volume risk.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A sustained run-lean shift below 54% pass rate.

rankings.rationale: 504 attempts with a rising late window (19.0 PPG) in a run-leaning offense (52.4% pass) whose backfield turned over -- attempt volume has more room up than down. Modest rush usage adds a small floor.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Pass rate staying under 52% with the new backfield.

rankings.rationale: 455 attempts plus genuinely useful rush production (66 carries, 323 yards, 5 TDs) at 17.8 PPG -- balanced volume in an offense returning its passing core minus Samuel's 127 vacated targets, which redistribute rather than disappear.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Rush usage vanishing (under 2 carries/game) alongside a healthy McCaffrey.

rankings.rationale: UP from v1.0 #14: the red-zone/rush-type data confirms an elite rushing floor -- designed_run_rate 0.67 and a 0.34 red-zone carry share, both top-of-pool, so his rushing is designed goal-line work, not scramble noise. Still capped by unverifiable NYJ starter status (pre-free-agency chart), which is why he stays mid-pack rather than rising into the dual-threat tier.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Confirmed 17-start runway (up 5+); a named backup role or benching risk (down 8+).

rankings.rationale: 562 attempts plus 81 carries for 489 yards as a rookie with ZERO rushing TDs -- volume that produced 15.0 PPG because efficiency lagged everywhere, and my methodology projects role-average efficiency onto held volume. The 154 vacated targets went to added receivers, not away from him.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: New-staff play volume below 57 plays/game, or his rush volume halving.

rankings.rationale: An 8-game season at a 286-attempt half-year pace (~600 full-season) in a 59.8%-pass offense -- the volume base is top-5 if health returns, but the sample is half a season and rush value is nil (13 carries). Projected on volume with a durability haircut.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A healthy 36+ attempt/game September restores a top-10 volume case (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: A 399-attempt 11-game season (36/game -- top-3 per-game pass volume) at 17.9 PPG late, with zero rush value and an availability record that is the whole risk. Volume when playing is not the question; games played is.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Nothing in-season -- this is an availability bet; a healthy first half moves him up 3-5.

rankings.rationale: 532 attempts of secure volume with a DECLINING late window (11.3 PPG) and no rush value -- role-average efficiency on his volume projects a rebound from 13.0, but the trend and Houston's negative passing EPA argue against more. Diggs' departure vacates 105 targets to unproven receivers.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A top-12 PPG September on the same volume signals the efficiency rebound arrived (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 421 rushing yards on 54 carries across a 13-game rookie season is real dual-threat opportunity; the passing volume (338 attempts, 80.5% snaps) has room to grow into a full season. New England's bottom-tier passing EPA and 57.4 plays/game cap the projection.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: 6+ carries/game with a 60%+ pass-rate offense would move him up 5+.

rankings.rationale: DOWN from v1.0 #16: the designed_run_rate is 0.026 -- his late-2024 rushing surge that v1.0 weighted was almost ENTIRELY scrambles, which my methodology treats as QB-play-quality-dependent and far less repeatable than designed carries. The rushing-floor thesis that lifted him in v1.0 does not survive the new data; his fantasy value reverts to a capped passing profile in a league-worst passing-EPA offense.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: The late-2024 rush usage disappearing in Weeks 1-3.

rankings.rationale: A 10-game injury season (284 attempts, 14.5 PPG) in the pool's lowest-volume offense, now under a new staff whose play volume is unknowable -- my stated blind spot. The 218 vacated targets mean the passing tree is being rebuilt around him, direction unknown.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: New-staff play volume above 62/game with him healthy (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 425 attempts in 15 games inside the pool's second-most run-leaning offense (47.7% pass) with no rush value and a declining late window (14.3 PPG) -- the volume structure itself caps him regardless of talent.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Green Bay's pass rate exceeding 53% sustainably.

rankings.rationale: 517 attempts of stable volume, zero rush value, 13.4 PPG output -- and 214 vacated targets (Kupp) replaced by an unproven-to-this-offense receiving corps. Pure-volume floor, no ceiling path in my data.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A 21+ PPG September on unchanged volume (efficiency, not opportunity, would be the driver -- and I'd stay skeptical).

rankings.rationale: No NFL usage data exists (2024 season missed) -- rookie-equivalent blind spot, confidence capped. Listed QB1 in a 54.5%-pass offense whose receiving corps returns intact; the rank is structure only.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Any early rushing usage signal (up 3-5); a quick hook risk materializing (down).

rankings.rationale: A 5-game, 105-attempt sample (8.8 PPG) with negligible rush value, listed QB1 over the benched veteran in a 62-plays/game offense -- the volume runway is real but the evidence base is the pool's thinnest among returning starters.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A 34+ attempt/game September at positive EPA (up 5+).
